verbGlossary.SCREENS BY: Prequalification evaluation filters bidders using {target} or quality metric as a pass/fail gate (e.g., subcontractor disqualified if TRIR exceeds owner threshold)
verbGlossary.WEIGHS: {source} scores or ranks proposals by factoring in {target}/safety metric (e.g., bid leveling sheet includes inspection pass rate as a weighted scoring criterion)
verbGlossary.OBLIGATES: {source} instrument imposes a contractual duty on the counterparty to participate in {target}/safety process (e.g., subcontract requires subcontractor to respond to safety observations within 24 hours)
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS: {source} instrument or procurement event initiates creation of {target}/safety record when a deviation is discovered (e.g., purchase order non-conforming delivery triggers a quality observation)
verbGlossary.ASSIGNS TO: {source} instrument designates the responsible party for resolving {target}/safety item (e.g., subcontract assigns punch item remediation to the trade contractor who installed the work)
verbGlossary.REQUIRES: {source} instrument or term makes {target}/safety deliverable a contractual prerequisite (e.g., purchase order requires material test reports before material acceptance)
verbGlossary.MANDATES: {source} or owner requirement compels execution of {target} or test at defined milestones (e.g., prime contract mandates third-party structural steel inspections per IBC 1705)
verbGlossary.SPECIFIES: {source} or contract exhibit defines the acceptance criteria, frequency, or protocol for {target}/safety activity (e.g., scope of work specifies inspection items for concrete placement)
verbGlossary.GOVERNS: {source} or contract terms establish the procedural rules and notification requirements for {target}/incident process (e.g., general conditions govern incident reporting timelines and escalation paths)
verbGlossary.PRESCRIBES: {source} terms define the required corrective response when {target}/safety event occurs (e.g., general conditions prescribe corrective action procedures for safety violations)
verbGlossary.TRIGGERED BY: {source} remedy or financial consequence is activated when {target}/safety deficiency remains unresolved (e.g., liquidated damages triggered by unresolved punch items past the contractual deadline)
verbGlossary.COVERS: {source} provision obligates the contractor to remedy {target} at no additional cost during the warranty period (e.g., warranty covers punch items discovered within 12 months of substantial completion)
verbGlossary.GATED BY: {source} milestone cannot be certified until {target}/safety metric meets the required threshold (e.g., substantial completion gated by punch item density falling below contractual limit)
verbGlossary.PACKAGES: {source}document assembles {target} or quality record into the final deliverable set required for project turnover (e.g., closeout package includes all material test reports and commissioning records)
verbGlossary.INVOKED BY: {source}is activated when {target} failure creates a claim exposure (e.g., builders risk policy invoked by a fire incident on the jobsite)
verbGlossary.EXPOSED BY: Bond or surety obligation faces potential claim when {target} failure indicates contractor default risk (e.g., performance bond exposed by persistent non-conformance reports suggesting inability to complete work)
verbGlossary.CORRELATES WITH: {source}/procurement metric trends in tandem with {target}/safety metric, indicating a shared root cause (e.g., rising change order rate correlates with increasing rework cost percentage)
verbGlossary.PENALIZES FOR: {source} or contract retention is withheld or increased based on the contractor's standing on {target}/safety metric (e.g., subcontractor retention rate adjusted when hazard recurrence rate exceeds acceptable threshold)
